title: Continued FuelEU Maritime pooling with Viking Line and Wallenius SOL, Continued FuelEU Maritime pooling with Viking Line and Wallenius SOL
summary: Viking Line and Wallenius SOL are pooling emissions credits under FuelEU Maritime using bio-LNG. Claiming "negative emissions" from livestock waste biogas is dubious without rigorous lifecycle analysis. Expect scrutiny as FuelEU Maritime fines approach for those relying on such claims.
content:
Their gas-powered vessels run on Gasum’s premium bio-LNG, which has negative emissions, meaning that the reduction is beyond 100%. This is possible when the biogas is produced using waste from livestock.

FuelEU Maritime came into force in 2025 and allows for a pooling mechanism, which means that vessels that exceed the regulation’s targets by using low- or zero-emission fuels can generate surplus compliance units on behalf of others. The units are used to offset deficit from other vessels within the same pool, providing flexibility and promoting broader adoption of low-emission fuels in the maritime sector.
